differentials.name: Coronal cleft =  failure of fusion of anterior + posterior ossification centers May be associated with:   premature male infant, Chondrodystrophia calcificans congenita
differentials.raw: Coronal cleft =  failure of fusion of anterior + posterior ossification centers May be associated with:   premature male infant, Chondrodystrophia calcificans congenita

differentials.name: Butterfly vertebra =  failure of fusion of lateral halves ← persistence of notochordal tissue
differentials.raw: Butterfly vertebra =  failure of fusion of lateral halves ← persistence of notochordal tissue

context: during 9th-12th week of gestation two ossification centers form for the ventral + dorsal half of vertebral body √  complete absence of vertebral body √  hypoplastic posterior elements may be present √  right / left hemivertebra √  scoliosis at birth (b)  Dorsal hemivertebra √  rapidly progressive kyphoscoliosis (c)  Ventral hemivertebra (extremely rare) Location:   usually in lower thoracic + lumbar spine √  vertical radiolucent band just behind midportion of vertebral body; disappears mostly by 6 months of life
